questions.question: The founder of the Mughal Empire in India.
The small principality in Transoxiana where Babur first became ruler.
The Governor of Punjab who invited Babur to invade India.
The Rajput king of Mewar who was defeated at the Battle of Khanua.
The Ottoman method of warfare used by Babur in India.
Babur's autobiography, which is a masterpiece of literature.
The treatise on Muslim law written by Babur.
The dynasty founded by Sher Shah after defeating Humayun.
The fort where Sher Shah died in an accidental blast of gun powder in 1545.
The system of branding horses with an imperial sign introduced by Sher Shah.
The famous road built by Sher Shah from Sonargoan to Peshawar.
The paper on which the amount of land revenue was recorded for peasants.
Humayun's sister who authored the Humayun Nama.
The silver coin of standard weight introduced by Sher Shah, used for centuries.
The new city built by Sher Shah on the banks of the river Yamuna near Delhi.
